questions.scenario: A file says a bucket should exist with production tags and public access blocked. What is this describing?
questions.explanation: Desired state describes the final shape the system should have after the tool finishes.

questions.scenario: Why is `make this fact true` usually safer than `always run this command`?

options.text: The tool can inspect reality and avoid unnecessary work.
Cloud APIs do not need permissions.

questions.explanation: Desired-state tools compare current reality with the intended fact before acting.

questions.scenario: Which items can be expressed as desired state?

options.text: A storage bucket exists with specific tags.
A service is enabled and running.
A config file has expected content and permissions.
An engineer remembers clicking the second tab.

questions.explanation: Desired state names facts about infrastructure or host configuration, not private memory of clicks.
